config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because once you give society priority by default, it can become an excuse to ignore personal freedom and dignity. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting individual rights first, while still limiting them only when there is a clear and necessary public interest. From my perspective, the balance should lean toward the individual, because majorities can be wrong and power is often abused.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu justru menjadi dasar agar negara dan mayoritas tidak seenaknya menginjak kebebasan, martabat, dan suara minoritas. Tentu ada keadaan tertentu, seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, ketika pembatasan masuk akal, tetapi itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau prioritasnya dibalik secara umum, saya khawatir itu terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, I am very aware that appeals to “the public good” can be used to pressure people to stay silent, obey unfair rules, or accept abuses that benefit those in power. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case, because in real life public health, safety, and social harmony sometimes do require limits. The key point is that society should justify any restriction very carefully, not assume it deserves priority by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat hak individu sebagai pagar utama agar negara, mayoritas, atau kelompok berkuasa tidak mudah mengorbankan kebebasan dan martabat orang yang lebih lemah. Argumen tentang kesehatan publik, keamanan, atau ketertiban memang sah, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasan harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan diberi prioritas umum yang terlalu luas. Kalau “demi masyarakat” dijadikan alasan default, menurut saya itu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be given automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, I have seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism, pressure conformity, and excuse unfair treatment, especially when power is not evenly distributed. I do think there are cases where individual rights must be limited for public health, safety, or order, but those limits should be narrow, transparent, and truly necessary. If society is always placed first by default, I believe that becomes too dangerous and too easy to abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu adalah batas penting agar mayoritas, negara, atau kelompok berkuasa tidak mudah mengorbankan kebebasan dan martabat orang yang lemah. Saya juga setuju dengan poin bahwa ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik dan keamanan, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasannya harus ketat, terukur, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan alasan default. Kalau “demi masyarakat” dipakai sebagai pembenaran utama, saya melihat risikonya terlalu besar untuk penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
